hook:
I automated my content distribution last month.

3 hours to set up.
5 hours saved every week.

Here's the exact stack (all free or cheap):
tweets:
The problem: I was posting manually to 4 platforms.

X, LinkedIn, Threads, newsletter.

Same content, reformatted slightly. 5-6 hours/week just copy-pasting.

That's 250+ hours/year on admin work.

Step 1: Content buffer in Notion.

I write once, tag by platform.

One database. Four views (one per platform).

Takes 10 minutes to set up.

Step 2: Typefully for X scheduling.

$12/month. Worth every penny.

I batch write 2 weeks of tweets on Sunday. Schedule them all.

Never touch X during the week unless replying.

Step 3: LinkedIn autopilot.

I use Taplio ($49/mo) to repurpose X content.

It rewrites tweets into LinkedIn format automatically.

Post 3x/week. Takes zero extra time.

Step 4: Newsletter on autopilot.

Beehiiv pulls from my content buffer via Zapier.

Every Friday, it auto-drafts a newsletter from that week's best posts.

I review for 5 minutes. Hit send.

Step 5: Threads cross-posting.

Typefully does this natively now.

One checkbox. Posts to both X and Threads.

Zero extra work.

The results after 30 days:

- X: +2,400 followers
- LinkedIn: +890 followers  
- Newsletter: +340 subs
- Time spent: 3 hours/week (down from 8)

Total cost: ~$61/month

Typefully: $12
Taplio: $49
Zapier: Free tier
Beehiiv: Free tier

ROI: 20 hours/month saved = ~$1,000 in time value if you bill at $50/hr

The key insight: automation isn't about being lazy.

It's about doing the creative work once and letting machines handle distribution.

Write good content. Let systems spread it.
cta:
Building more systems like this every week.

Follow @PRINTMAXXER for the playbook.

DM me "AUTO" if you want the Notion template.
